title: Crumbly Russian Floral Pryanik
description: A crumbly, spicy, and sweet monsoon-inspired Russian dessert featuring Chinese five-spice powder, caramelized sugar, and a hint of ayurvedic spices.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the sugar, honey, and butter. Mix until smooth.
Add the Chinese five-spice powder, cardamom powder, and ginger powder to the bowl and mix well.
Add the rose petals and rosewater to the bowl and mix until the petals are evenly distributed.
Add the almond flour and eggs to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth.
Roll the dough out to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Use a cookie cutter or a glass to cut out shapes from the dough.
Place the shapes on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly golden.
Allow the pryanik to cool completely on the baking sheet before serving.
Store the pryanik in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
